evidence.result: 4 test files passed, 24 tests passed; includes module guard and tenant portal service coverage
TypeScript build passed
2 test files passed, 36 tests passed
Vite production build completed
Backend tests/build and frontend tests/build passed after Phase 3 contract tab foundation changes
SQLite applied baseline, module entitlement, and portal config migrations; tenant_portal_configs schema exists
PASS_WITH_NOTES; no blocking security or tenant isolation issues; portal loading UX note fixed after audit
2 Playwright tests passed in system Google Chrome: enterprise login and core module navigation; developer login and tenant management navigation
PASS_WITH_NOTES; no blocking issue; confirmed temporary SQLite DB, test-only JWT_SECRET, system Chrome channel, and honest baseline smoke scope

Contract management page now has construction/procurement/subcontract tabs. Subcontract list uses /api/contracts/sub-contracts so contract management remains a default base feature and does not depend on labor module entitlement.
Backend tests 4 files / 24 tests passed; backend TypeScript build passed; frontend tests 2 files / 36 tests passed; frontend production build passed after Phase 3 tab foundation

2 Playwright tests passed in system Google Chrome after Phase 3 tab foundation; enterprise smoke clicks contract management and all three contract tabs, plus core enabled modules; developer smoke opens tenant management
PASS; no blocking route-order, tenant-isolation, module-entitlement, or fake-completion issues. Non-blocking loading-state suggestion fixed after audit.

Contract detail attachments now use contract-owned APIs for list/upload/authenticated download/delete. Frontend no longer uses laborApi or raw /uploads links for contract attachments. Backend enforces tenant scoping, PDF/image file whitelist, 50MB limit, JSON upload errors, and download rate limiting.
Backend tests 4 files / 24 tests passed; backend TypeScript build passed; frontend tests 2 files / 36 tests passed; frontend production build passed after contract attachment slice

2 Playwright tests passed in system Google Chrome. Enterprise smoke creates a contract, opens details, uploads a PDF fixture, downloads it, deletes the attachment, then deletes the test contract; it also clicks all contract tabs and core modules. Developer smoke opens tenant management.
PASS; tenant isolation and labor-module decoupling confirmed. Claude found a smoke upload race and JSON upload error handling issue; both were fixed before final verify/browser smoke.

Procurement contracts can link to construction contracts, create payment records, and manage categorized invoice/payment-voucher attachments. Backend enforces tenant-scoped parent contract validation and procurement-only payment records.
Backend tests 4 files / 24 tests passed; backend TypeScript build passed; frontend tests 2 files / 36 tests passed; frontend production build passed after procurement contract workflow slice
Prisma schema is valid after ContractPayment and parentContractId index declarations

2 Playwright tests passed in system Google Chrome. Enterprise smoke creates a construction contract, uploads/downloads/deletes its attachment, creates a linked procurement contract, creates a payment record, uploads/downloads/deletes a procurement invoice and payment voucher, then deletes test contracts; developer smoke opens tenant management.

PASS; BLOCKERS none. Claude reviewed tenant isolation, approval-free procurement workflow, attachment category separation, browser smoke realism, and schema/migration consistency. Non-blocking updateContract/type notes were fixed after audit and reverified.

Graph built 130 files / 1413 nodes / 14414 edges. detect-changes risk score 0.50, mainly because backend contract service functions have no direct unit tests; real Chrome smoke covers the procurement end-to-end path.

Subcontract contracts can link to construction contracts and work teams, create payment records, and manage categorized payment-voucher/settlement-voucher attachments. Backend exposes these under the default contract module without relying on wms/labor module entitlement.
Backend tests 4 files / 24 tests passed; backend TypeScript build passed; frontend tests 2 files / 36 tests passed; frontend production build passed after subcontract contract workflow slice
Prisma schema is valid after SubContract.workTeamId and WorkTeam.subContracts relation declarations

2 Playwright tests passed in system Google Chrome. Enterprise smoke creates construction, procurement, and subcontract contracts; subcontract flow selects a work team, creates a payment record, uploads/downloads/deletes payment voucher and settlement voucher fixtures, then deletes the subcontract. Developer smoke opens tenant management.

PASS; BLOCKERS none. Claude reviewed tenant isolation, contract-module independence from wms/labor entitlement, approval-free subcontract workflow, attachment category separation, browser smoke coverage, and schema/migration consistency. Stale empty-state copy was fixed after audit and reverified.

Graph built 131 files / 1432 nodes / 14902 edges. detect-changes risk score 0.50, mainly because backend contract service functions have no direct unit tests; real Chrome smoke covers the subcontract end-to-end path.

evidence.command: implementation + real browser personnel face/mobile check-in/anomaly/trusted-location
evidence.result: Added MiniProgramConfig, AttendanceSetting, MobileCheckInRecord, TrustedCheckInLocation, personnel face fields, /api/mobile/check-in, labor admin endpoints, developer default and tenant mini-program config UI, personnel face upload UI, and attendance mobile check-in/anomaly/trusted-location UI.
Backend tests 4 files / 24 tests passed; backend TypeScript build passed; frontend tests 2 files / 36 tests passed; frontend production build passed after mobile check-in slice.
Prisma schema is valid after mini-program, attendance setting, mobile check-in, trusted location, and personnel face schema additions.

2 Playwright tests passed in system Google Chrome. Enterprise smoke creates a personnel record, uploads face photo, sets daily check-in rule to two times, posts mobile check-ins through /api/mobile/check-in, verifies abnormal county detection, batch-resolves an abnormal record, and adds a personal trusted check-in location. Existing contract upload/download/delete flows still pass. Developer smoke opens tenant management.

PASS; no blocking issues. Claude confirmed data model, appId/phone tenant routing, multiple-tenant phone conflict handling, labor module rejection, non-blocking abnormal check-in, batch resolve, trusted location, frontend admin entry points, and real Chrome coverage. Yellow notes: face provider is stub for MVP; trusted location list/delete and photo previews are UX follow-ups.

Graph built 132 files / 1458 nodes / 15388 edges. detect-changes analyzed 42 changed files with risk score 0.50; direct backend unit-test gaps remain, while current real Chrome smoke covers the end-to-end mobile check-in path.

evidence.command: npm --prefix backend audit --audit-level=high || true && npm --prefix frontend audit --audit-level=high || true
evidence.result: Backend: 15 vulnerabilities (6 high). Frontend: 8 vulnerabilities (6 high). Existing dependency debt recorded; not fixed in this feature slice to avoid unscoped breaking upgrades.

known_yellow_items: npm audit high vulnerabilities remain as technical debt from baseline; not resolved in Phase 2.

Prisma migrate deploy to a temporary SQLite DB previously returned an opaque Schema engine error while direct sqlite3 SQL execution succeeded; keep migration deployment as a Yellow follow-up before production rollout.
Independent login page DNS ownership/resolve verification and logo upload picker are not implemented in Phase 2 MVP.
Browser E2E for independent-domain portal login and unopen-module 403/menu-hidden matrix is still required before Product Green.
Browser smoke harness uses raw SQL migrations because Prisma migrate/db push returns an opaque Schema engine error in this environment.
Browser smoke screenshots use explicit docs/smoke-evidence paths; future full regression should use testInfo/outputDir for richer artifact grouping.

Contract attachment deletion is currently a hard delete because the existing Attachment schema has no isActive/deletedAt field; soft-delete attachment audit trail remains a follow-up if required by production policy.

Face recognition provider is currently a local stub adapter; production rollout must choose and configure a real third-party face recognition service or explicitly accept manual/provider_error review.
Trusted check-in locations can be added from abnormal records, but a dedicated list/delete management UI and photo preview UI remain UX follow-ups.
npm audit high vulnerabilities remain as dependency technical debt; backend and frontend audit outputs were recorded after this slice.
notes: Phase 4 mini-program/personnel face/mobile check-in/anomaly/trusted-location V1 is implemented and verified with unified build/tests, Prisma validate, Claude CLI audit, code-review-graph, npm audit recording, and real Chrome smoke. Human production decision still required for third-party face recognition provider and npm audit dependency upgrades.
